title: Gently Poached Japanese Shungiku Pasta
description: A delicate and flavorful pescatarian-friendly dish featuring shungiku, perfect for a weeknight dinner, with a silky texture and herbal notes.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the spaghetti according to package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the garlic and ginger until fragrant.
Add the shungiku to the skillet and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce and sesame oil, and pour the mixture over the shungiku.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Poach the shrimp in simmering water until pink and cooked through, about 2 minutes.
Drain the spaghetti and add it to the skillet with the shungiku mixture, tossing to combine.
To serve, place the spaghetti on a plate and top with poached shrimp.
Garnish with additional shungiku if desired.
